purpose: Early-warning system for EM currency crises using a five-signal scoring methodology.
components: Signal scoring: 5 signals (specific signals not enumerated in synthesis)
Crisis probability: 80% at 4-5 signals, 45% at 3 signals, 10% at 0-2 signals
False positive rate: 25-30% (typically from preemptive policy adjustment)
Regime type as severity determinant: managed/pegged produce 30-60% discrete devaluations vs continuous adjustment in credible floaters
current_assessment: Validated across 15+ episodes since 1980. More actionable and better validated than alternatives per pair_0 debate. Stronger for detection than timing: knowing 3+ signals present narrows crisis window only to within 24 months. Currently 8-12 economies at elevated risk.
limitations: Timing precision limited to within 24 months window
25-30% false positive rate from preemptive adjustment
Specific 5 signals not fully enumerated in available synthesis
Cannot distinguish which of 8-12 elevated-risk economies will actually experience crisis
Framework may cause its own failure if preemptive adjustment prevents crisis
